items.description:
Resolution - Approving the Appointment of Various Persons as Commissioners of
Deeds for 2025/2026.

Agreement — With Onondaga Earth Corps (OEC) to provide training, programming
and supervision to youth hired for the summer and to collaboratively run the “Parks
Corp” and “Clean Streets Crew” as part of the City youth employment and service
program, for the period of July 1, 2026 — June 30, 2027.

Resolution — Memorializing the United States Senators Schumer and Gillibrand,
Congressman Mannion, Governor Hochul, the New York State Assembly and the
New York State Senate to act to protect voting rights, regarding the “Voting Rights
Act” and how congressional districts are created.

Authorize — The Extension of the 90-day Street Closure of Comstock Avenue
between Waverly Avenue and Marshall Street, to run from March 30,.2026-June 28,
2026, for construction staging for Syracuse University resident hall project.

Authorize — The 2026/2027 Unimproved Street Program (Slurry Seal), on behalf of
the Department of Public Works, for the streets as listed in Appendix “A”. Cost
thereof to be charged to the premises fronting thereon. Charge the proceeds from
the sale of bonds. Total cost not to exceed $1,800,000.

Resolution — Urging the Governor.of New York, the New York Senate, and the New
York State Assembly.to support NYS Senate Bill S.5684 and the Assembly Bill
A.6543, by passing the Bigger, Better Bottle Bill.
f
Amend — Ord.

Amend — Ord #667 (07/25/2025), “Agreement — With ComSource, for consulting
services on a need basis, on behalf of Information Technology, for the period of July

2025 — June 30, 2026, (or until a new RFP is awarded). Total cost not to exceed
$100,000 charged to Account #541500.01.16800. The Mayor waived the RFP
process.” Amend to increase the amount by $45,000 and extend the contract to
expire August 31, 2026. All other terms and conditions remain the same.
organizations: Pioneer Companies, to rent space inside the State Tower building, to use

items.description:
Resolution — In recognition of Tourette Syndrome Awareness Month, in support of an
effort to raise awareness and offer encouragement to individuals and families
throughout the community. —

Accept - From Eric Hart, owner of First Light Syracuse Goods, an in-kind donation of
12 Syracuse City Flags, (valued at $259:20) to each of our 11 Fire Stations and
Training Division headquarters, as a show of appreciation for all the work the SFD
does to protect our community.
organizations: Eric Hart, owner of First Light Syracuse Goods

items.description:
Appropriate Funds — From Special Objects of Expense Account #595850.01.90000
an amount not to exceed $21,000, as approved in FY2026 Budget, and authorize a
one (1) year contract with the Erie Canal Museum for operation and management of
the Syracuse Urban Cultural Park (Heritage Area) Visitor Center...

Authorize - The Commissioner of Finance to issue Refunding Bonds, through the
Syracuse Industrial Development Agency, in a principle amount not to exceed
$66,920,000 to refund the Joint School Construction Board the following bonds:
Series 2018A and 2018B, which is expected to generate an estimated ...
organizations: Counsel Press, printing services for certain Appellate Court submissions

items.description:
Resolution — Urging the Governor of New York State, the New York State Senate,
and the New York State Assembly to support NYS Senate Bills S.8004, S.8623,
S.8872; and Assembly Bills A.6211 and A.

Permission — To Manager Gordon Clarkson on behalf of the Century Club, owner of
the property located at 480 James Street to encroach approximately 14’ for a historic
monument pole sign into the James Street right-of-way.
Amend — Ord.

Special Permit — To approve a “Commercial Food Preparation” business on the
property located at 3001-3023 James Street. No one spoke in favor of or in
opposition to the proposal. Annas Almaletti, Zaman Bakery, applicant. Eastwood
Maliti’s LLC, owner.
